_meta.title: Value Compass - Scored Maker Dataset
_meta.polarity: ALL axes scored 0-4 where HIGHER = more pro-social (more transparent, better culture/ESG, more labour & supply-chain integrity, more dispersed ownership, more public wealth-sharing). A high-scoring maker draws a large polygon on the compass.

confidence.A: strong / externally validated (e.g., FMTI score exists)
confidence.B: moderate / some primary disclosure or credible reporting

axis_aliases.wealth_dispersion: your 'wealth distribution' axis, higher = more dispersed

key_anchors.transparency: Stanford FMTI 2025 (avg 40.69/100; IBM 95 high; xAI & Midjourney 14 low). 0-4 mapping: 0=<=12, 1=13-29, 2=30-49, 3=50-74, 4=>=75.
key_anchors.environmental_note: FMTI 2025 found 10 of 13 firms disclose NOTHING on energy/carbon/water - the environmental sub-indicator is near-zero sector-wide.
key_anchors.labour_benchmark: Global-South data labelers commonly earn ~$1.50-2/hr; OpenAI/Sama Kenya is the canonical documented case.

_meta.products_note: products_models are listed inside each maker node for filtering/comparison; they are NOT separate nodes.
_meta.join_key: Each maker has an 'id' (short name). funders[].makers_backed and owns_outright reference makers by this id. Join on id, NOT on display 'name'.
_meta.capital_profile_note: Each maker has a factual 'capital_profile' (objective attributes). The Capital Lens (user-configurable) decides which attributes count as concerns; it is NOT a fixed 6th score.
_meta.last_freshness_review: 2026-06-13: verified xAI/SpaceX (closed Feb 2026), Anthropic ($965B Series H + confidential IPO), OpenAI (Oct 2025 recapitalization), Cohere/Aleph Alpha (announced Apr 2026), DeepSeek (~$7.4B round near closing + V4), Meta (Muse Spark closed-weight pivot Apr 2026), Moonshot (~$10B+). Tool-layer valuations are date-stamped (late 2025-early 2026) and were not re-verified individually.

makers.name: Anthropic
makers.structure: Public Benefit Corporation; Long-Term Benefit Trust
makers.stated_values: Safety-first, beneficial AI at the frontier; Constitutional AI; interpretability.
makers.tension_hook: Safety-first framing vs. participating in the same capability/compute race it warns about.

transparency.note: Most transparent frontier lab in FMTI 2025 (rose to 2nd of the 6 longitudinal firms) but still only industry-typical; opaque on data/compute/environment.

culture_esg.note: Strong mission-aligned culture and unusually robust governance (PBC + Long-Term Benefit Trust); but zero environmental disclosure like all peers.

labour_integrity.note: Relies on outsourced RLHF/labeling vendors with no public disclosure; no major documented abuses, but supply chain is opaque.

wealth_dispersion.note: Economics heavily concentrated in Amazon (~15-19%) and Google (~14%); the trust governs mission, not the cap table.

public_sharing.note: PBC + Long-Term Benefit Trust as binding structure; founders signed the Giving Pledge; paid-access model limits free reach.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: OpenAI
makers.structure: For-profit PBC (OpenAI Group) controlled by the nonprofit OpenAI Foundation; recapitalized Oct 2025 (capped-profit model scrapped)
makers.stated_values: Ensure AGI benefits all of humanity; broad distribution of benefits.
makers.tension_hook: 'Benefit all humanity' nonprofit origins vs. for-profit restructuring, founder departures, and commercial pace.

transparency.note: FMTI 2025 middle group but declined ~14 points; near the bottom of the firms tracked all three years; limited o3 disclosure.

culture_esg.note: Repeated governance turmoil (2023 board crisis, safety-team departures) and a contested for-profit conversion, completed Oct 2025; environmental non-disclosure.

labour_integrity.note: Documented Kenya/Sama case: moderators paid ~$2/hr to review violent content with inadequate support.

wealth_dispersion.note: After the Oct 2025 recapitalization the nonprofit OpenAI Foundation holds ~26% and controls the board, Microsoft ~27%, and ~47% sits with employees and investors - control rests with the nonprofit and economics are moderately spread (no single holder >27%), not capped.

public_sharing.note: The nonprofit OpenAI Foundation controls the for-profit PBC and holds a ~$130B equity stake earmarked for philanthropy (health, AI resilience); but the capped-profit -> PBC reversal is a live stated-vs-observed gap.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Google DeepMind
makers.stated_values: Solve intelligence to advance science and benefit humanity; responsible AI.
makers.tension_hook: Research-for-humanity mission vs. ad-revenue parent and enterprise-productivity pressure.

transparency.note: FMTI 2025 middle group; criticized for delayed Gemini model cards/technical reports.

culture_esg.note: Alphabet publishes mature corporate sustainability reporting; offset by founder dual-class voting control.

labour_integrity.note: Opaque rater supply chain; vendor-rater pay/condition complaints reported across Google's contractors.

wealth_dispersion.note: Wholly owned by Alphabet; founders Page and Brin hold >51% of voting power via dual-class shares.

public_sharing.note: Open Gemma weights and Google.org philanthropy, but no AI-specific public-benefit/windfall structure.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: xAI
makers.jurisdiction: US (PBC; now a SpaceX subsidiary; TX/CA)
makers.structure: PBC; now a wholly owned SpaceX subsidiary
makers.stated_values: Understand the true nature of the universe; maximally truth-seeking; anti-censorship.
makers.tension_hook: 'Maximally truth-seeking/neutral' claim vs. owner control, X integration, and model-steering episodes.

transparency.note: Tied for the lowest score in FMTI 2025 (14/100); discloses almost nothing about data, model, or practices.

culture_esg.note: Operated dozens of unpermitted gas turbines in a predominantly Black South Memphis community; sued under the Clean Air Act by the NAACP/SELC/Earthjustice.

labour_integrity.note: Minimal labour disclosure; rapid build-out with little visibility into data work.

wealth_dispersion.note: Elon Musk holds supermajority control; xAI was absorbed into Musk-controlled SpaceX.

public_sharing.note: PBC in form only; no substantive public-sharing mechanism; Grok is paid/closed.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Meta (AI)
makers.structure: Public; founder retains majority voting via dual-class
makers.stated_values: Historically open-weight (Llama) to democratize access, but pivoted to a closed, proprietary frontier model (Muse Spark, Apr 2026); 'personal superintelligence.'
makers.tension_hook: Open-weight 'democratization' rhetoric vs. the April 2026 pivot to a closed, proprietary frontier model and the earlier Llama 4 benchmark-integrity issues.

transparency.note: FMTI 2025 score halved (60->31); released no technical report for Llama 4 — openness of weights did not equal transparency. Now also shipping the closed-weight Muse Spark (Apr 2026).

culture_esg.note: 2025 AI-org turmoil (LeCun departure, Llama 4 benchmark-integrity allegations, $100M poaching); founder voting control; corporate sustainability reporting exists.

labour_integrity.note: Holds ~49% of Scale AI; documented Kenyan content-moderator litigation over pay and trauma.

wealth_dispersion.note: Zuckerberg retains voting control via dual-class; AI effort is self-funded by Meta.

public_sharing.note: Still releases Llama as open weights (a public good), but pivoted to closed, proprietary Muse Spark at the frontier (Apr 2026), narrowing the public-benefit claim; no profit-sharing commitment.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Mistral AI
makers.stated_values: Open, portable, efficient models; European AI sovereignty.
makers.tension_hook: Open/sovereign-Europe positioning vs. reliance on US backers and a Dutch strategic lead investor.

transparency.note: FMTI 2025 score fell by more than two-thirds (to 18/100) despite open weights.

culture_esg.note: Operates under stronger EU labour norms, but publishes little ESG detail.

labour_integrity.note: Smaller footprint and EU context; labeling supply chain not disclosed.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founders plus diverse VCs; ASML is largest shareholder (~11%) but no single majority.

public_sharing.note: Open-weight releases and a European public-interest/sovereignty framing; no profit pledge.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: DeepSeek
founders: Liang Wenfeng (2023) - also founder of High-Flyer
lead_backers: National AI Industry Investment Fund (round near closing, Jun 2026)
makers.structure: Private; China-based; first-ever external round (~$7.4B led by Tencent & CATL, with the state-backed National AI Industry Fund) near closing as of June 2026
makers.stated_values: Open-source, research-driven AGI for all; radical cost efficiency.
makers.tension_hook: Open/for-all framing vs. Chinese data-governance and state-adjacent funding context.

transparency.note: FMTI 2025 middle group; open weights but opaque on data and training — openness is not transparency.

culture_esg.note: No ESG disclosure; quant-fund parent culture; little public information.

labour_integrity.note: Opaque; Chinese data-labour context with no disclosure.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founder Liang Wenfeng controls ~89.5% via the High-Flyer parent.

public_sharing.note: Gives models away as open weights at very low or zero cost — strong de facto public benefit, though no formal pledge.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Microsoft
makers.jurisdiction: US (Washington state; widely held public co.)
lead_backers: also the ~$13B+ lead backer of OpenAI
makers.structure: Public; dual role - maker (MAI/Copilot) AND funder (OpenAI)
makers.stated_values: Empower every person and organization to achieve more; 'humanist superintelligence.'
makers.tension_hook: Funds OpenAI while shipping competing MAI models - edge points at OpenAI both ways.

transparency.note: Not in FMTI 2025; publishes an annual Responsible AI Transparency Report and model cards, but MAI disclosure is new/limited.

culture_esg.note: Established sustainability reporting and a carbon-negative-by-2030 pledge; offset by datacenter energy/water strain and layoffs.

labour_integrity.note: Uses content-moderation/data vendors; some historical moderator-harm reporting; limited disclosure.

wealth_dispersion.note: Widely held public company with no founder control bloc — the most dispersed ownership in the set.

public_sharing.note: Large corporate philanthropy and broad affordable access; no AI-specific public-benefit structure.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Perplexity
makers.tension_hook: Accuracy/cited-sources promise vs. publisher disputes over content sourcing.

transparency.note: Not FMTI-scored; limited disclosure on how it sources and ranks answers; runs on others' models.

culture_esg.note: Young startup; no major culture scandals; little ESG disclosure.

labour_integrity.note: Inherits upstream model-supply-chain labour; publisher disputes over content sourcing.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founders plus many VCs and strategics (Nvidia, SoftBank, Bezos); no single majority.

public_sharing.note: Free tier exists; no structural public-benefit commitment.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Midjourney
makers.stated_values: Expand human imagination and creativity; independence.
makers.tension_hook: No funder values to map (the outlier) vs. artist-consent / copyright litigation.

transparency.note: Tied for the lowest FMTI 2025 score (14/100); no model or data disclosure.

culture_esg.note: Tiny profitable team; no ESG disclosure; no notable workplace scandal.

labour_integrity.note: Sued by Disney/Universal (2025) and a 2023 artists' class action over uncompensated training on creators' work.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founder-owned with no outside equity — concentrated in one person, though free of any backer bloc.

public_sharing.note: Paid-only, closed, no open weights or public-benefit commitment.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: ElevenLabs
makers.stated_values: Make content accessible in any voice and language; ethical audio AI with safeguards.
makers.tension_hook: Consent / anti-misuse safeguards vs. voice-cloning and deepfake abuse cases.

transparency.note: Not FMTI-scored; closed models; discloses some safety/consent tooling but little else.

culture_esg.note: Young startup; voice-misuse governance questions; little ESG disclosure.

labour_integrity.note: Voice-actor consent concerns balanced by some consent and provenance tooling.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founders plus a broad VC syndicate (a16z, ICONIQ, Sequoia); no single majority.

public_sharing.note: Free tier; no structural public-benefit commitment.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Canva
makers.stated_values: Empower everyone to design; 'two-step plan' - succeed then do good.
makers.tension_hook: 'Do good' mission vs. scale, creator-displacement concerns, and AI training-data questions.

transparency.note: Not a foundation-model developer; standard SaaS disclosure but limited AI-stack transparency.

culture_esg.note: Strong workplace and social reputation; Pledge 1% and Canva Foundation; comparatively light emitter.

labour_integrity.note: Pays a large contributor community for content — a comparatively better creator-compensation posture.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founder stakes significant but broad employee ownership and a wide investor base.

public_sharing.note: Founders pledged ~30% of their equity to charity (Pledge 1%) and offer free education/nonprofit tiers — best-in-class among the tools.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Lovable
makers.stated_values: Let anyone build software in natural language; democratize software creation.
makers.tension_hook: 'Anyone can build' vs. security/quality of generated apps and dependence on frontier-model APIs.

transparency.note: App layer built on frontier APIs; minimal disclosure about its own stack.

culture_esg.note: Very young, fast-scaling startup; little public information.

labour_integrity.note: Inherited exposure: depends on frontier-model APIs whose upstream labour it does not control.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founder-heavy early cap table plus Accel/CapitalG and others.

public_sharing.note: Free tier; no structural public-benefit commitment.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Replit
makers.stated_values: Bring the next billion software creators online; accessible coding and agents.
makers.tension_hook: Empowerment/accessibility mission vs. agent-reliability incidents and monetization-shift friction.

transparency.note: Developer-community openness, but its AI agent stack is largely opaque; not FMTI-scored.

culture_esg.note: Startup; some past monetization/community friction; little ESG disclosure.

labour_integrity.note: Inherited exposure via frontier-model APIs; little direct data-labour.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founders plus a16z/Coatue/QIA and others; no single majority.

public_sharing.note: Education and free-access mission ('next billion creators'); no profit-sharing structure.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Gamma
makers.stated_values: Reimagine how ideas are presented; AI-native, design-free creation.
makers.tension_hook: Lean/efficient ethos vs. quality-vs-volume questions and frontier-API dependence.

transparency.note: App layer on frontier APIs; minimal disclosure about its own stack.

culture_esg.note: Lean, profitable, small team; little public ESG information.

public_sharing.note: Free tier; no structural public-benefit commitment.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Cursor (Anysphere)
makers.stated_values: Make software engineering dramatically faster; AI-native coding for professional developers.
makers.tension_hook: 'Empower developers' built entirely on Anthropic/OpenAI models it competes with and depends on.

transparency.note: Closed product built on frontier APIs; minimal disclosure about its own stack.

labour_integrity.note: Inherited exposure: depends on Anthropic/OpenAI models whose upstream labour it does not control.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founders plus Accel/Coatue/Thrive/a16z; no single majority.

public_sharing.note: Paid product; no structural public-benefit commitment.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Cohere
founders: Aidan Gomez (CEO, co-author of the Transformer paper)
makers.structure: Private; Canada; announced April 2026 acquisition of Germany's Aleph Alpha (~$20B combined entity; $600M Schwarz Group financing), expected to close later 2026
makers.stated_values: Secure, private, enterprise-grade AI; customer data stays with the customer; sovereign deployment.
makers.tension_hook: 'Sovereign/private' positioning vs. US chipmaker capital (Nvidia, AMD) and the April 2026 Aleph Alpha acquisition consolidating European sovereign AI.

transparency.note: Not in FMTI 2025; publishes model cards and emphasizes audited enterprise outputs.

culture_esg.note: Enterprise/governed-AI positioning; limited public ESG disclosure.

labour_integrity.note: Trains largely on licensed/enterprise data; less creator-scraping controversy.

wealth_dispersion.note: Founders plus Radical/Inovia and strategics (Nvidia, AMD, Salesforce).

public_sharing.note: Runs Cohere For AI, a nonprofit open research lab, and releases the open multilingual Aya models.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.

makers.name: Moonshot AI (Kimi)
makers.structure: Private; China; ~$10B+ valuation (Feb 2026, up from $4.3B Series C in Dec 2025); IPO planned H2 2026
makers.stated_values: Long-context AGI; open-weight Kimi models; consumer + developer access.
makers.tension_hook: Open-weights / 'AGI for all' framing vs. Alibaba/Tencent strategic control and Chinese data-governance context.

transparency.note: Not FMTI-scored; open-weight Kimi models but opaque practices, similar to DeepSeek.

culture_esg.note: Little public information; no ESG disclosure.

labour_integrity.note: Opaque; Chinese data-labour context with no disclosure.

wealth_dispersion.note: Strategic concentration via Alibaba and Tencent backing.

public_sharing.note: Open-weight Kimi releases plus free consumer access; no formal pledge.

capital_profile.note: Factual capital attributes (objective). Whether any of these is a 'concern' is the user's call via the Capital Lens.
